$comment: GECR (Gate Evidence Cross-Reference Protocol) — JSON schema for .claude/patterns/gate-evidence-rules.json. Used at runtime by gate_evidence_runner.py via jsonschema.validate() at load time. Malformed rules raise loud SystemExit(2). Closes #1473 + #1470 root cause (gates enforce structural shape as proxy for semantic property) by binding gate pass-signals to evidence sources external to the validated artifact.
title: Gate Evidence Cross-Reference Rules

evidence_check_intentionally_structural.description: List of check identifiers (format: <rel-path>:<lineno>) that are intentionally structural-shape — exempt from rule_gate_verdict_evidence_coverage. Each entry should include a justification (string or {check_id, justification} object form).

always_included_paths.description: Paths always included regardless of path_glob — used when an audited file (e.g., nav-bar.tsx) must appear in scope even if path_glob would not naturally include it

params.description: Matcher-specific parameters. Validated by the matcher implementation, not by this schema.

id.description: Stable rule identifier; kebab-case. Used by --rule-id CLI argument.

type.description: Rule type. Each type maps to specific matcher kinds and predicate behaviors.

gate_id.description: Gate this rule serves (e.g., bg2-wire-check-1, check-observation-artifacts). Used in failure citations.

failure_citation_format.description: Template string for failure citations. Available placeholders depend on matcher kind.

mode_env.description: Environment variable name for MODE override (warn|deny). Default warn during soak per #1291 convention.

schema_cutoff.description: If true, rule honors schema_version_gate.required_schema_version(rid) — pre-cutoff runs SKIP (exit 0) instead of evaluating.

exempt_paths.description: Optional path globs to exclude from evidence scan.
